summary: Low-wing, single-engine, fixed-pitch, fixed gear — like the 172 it flies on throttle + mixture, but it adds two habits the Cessna doesn't teach: carburetor heat and switching fuel tanks (LEFT/RIGHT only, no BOTH).
aircraft_notes: Fixed-pitch prop: leave the Bravo's blue prop lever unbound. Fixed gear: leave the gear lever unbound. The Archer's carburetor ices — carb heat gets a dedicated switch and shows up in the Descent, Approach, Engine Roughness and Engine Failure checklists. The fuel selector has NO 'BOTH' position: you must switch tanks in cruise (checklist: 'Fuel selector — switch tanks as needed') and select the FULLEST tank before landing, so keep it mouse-clicked on the panel where you can see it. The electric fuel pump is ON for every takeoff and landing.
coaching: Create a separate 'PA-28' controls profile per device in Options → Controls so the Archer's carb-heat and fuel-pump bindings don't collide with your 172 profile.
Sensitivity: the Archer is lighter in roll than the 172 — around -25% on ailerons, -30% on pitch, small dead zones.
Before Start with hardware: BAT on (Alpha), fuel selector DESIRED TANK (mouse — look at it!), then Engine Start: fuel pump ON (Bravo switch), mixture full rich (red lever), crank with the rotary.
Run-up at 2000 RPM (not 1800 like the 172): mags max 175 drop/50 diff, carb heat ON then OFF — watch for the RPM drop that proves it works.
Takeoff: fuel pump ON, flaps up (normal) or 25° (short field), rotate 55-60 KIAS, climb Vy 76 KIAS.
Cruise: fuel pump OFF, lean the red lever, and set a timer — switch tanks every 30 minutes so the wings stay balanced.
Approach (the Archer flow): carb heat ON as required, fullest tank, mixture rich, fuel pump ON, landing light ON — then flaps a notch per leg, 70 KIAS final, 65 over the numbers.
Go-around: full throttle, carb heat OFF (cold), flaps to 25° immediately, climb 65+, then bleed the flaps up slowly — the Archer sinks if you dump them.

honeycomb_alpha.usage_tip: The low wing rolls quicker than a 172 — smaller inputs.
Trim to 76 KIAS in the climb and it will hold it hands-off.
Off before the approach — the checklist assumes you hand-fly the landing.
The low wing hides traffic in turns — lift a wing and look before turning.
'Master switch — ON' in preflight, OFF in Emergency Landing memory items.
OFF/ON cycle is the Low Voltage Light procedure.
ON after start, OFF before shutdown ('Electrical equipment / avionics — OFF').
ON at Run-up ('Landing light — ON'), OFF in Climb/Cruise, ON again for Approach.
Wing Fire memory item turns these OFF.
'Strobes — ON' in the Run-up checklist, before taking the runway.

honeycomb_alpha.msfs_setting: MAGNETO OFF / RIGHT / LEFT / BOTH / START (per position)
honeycomb_alpha.usage_tip: Run-up mag check: 175 max drop, 50 max difference. Engine Roughness checklist: L — R — BOTH on this rotary.

honeycomb_bravo.usage_tip: 800-1000 RPM after start, 2000 for run-up, full for takeoff, 1700 abeam the numbers (75 KIAS).
The Archer II has no prop lever.
Full rich for start and landing, lean for taxi and cruise, idle cut-off shuts the engine down.
Full power instantly for the go-around, then carb heat OFF with the switch below.
The Archer's trim is between the seats like this wheel — retrim after every flap notch.
Three notches (10/25/40). The real Archer uses a Johnson bar — pull decisively, one notch per pattern leg.
'U — Undercarriage: fixed, verify' — the checklist item is a look, not a lever.
Cruise workload relief while you run tank-switch timers.
Heading bug flying for VFR navigation practice.
Track VORs — the Archer II panel is classic radio-nav training.
Hold altitude while leaning and switching tanks.

honeycomb_bravo.msfs_setting: HEADING BUG INC/DEC, AP ALT VAR INC/DEC
honeycomb_bravo.usage_tip: Bind with the rotary selector in HDG and ALT positions.
ON for start, takeoff and landing; OFF in cruise with a pressure check — it's in almost every Archer checklist.
ON before reducing power for descent, ON at the first sign of engine roughness — the Archer's signature emergency habit.
Preflight test; IFR item in the run-up.

velocityone_rudder.usage_tip: The Archer needs less rudder than the 172 but still wants it in the climb; slips work beautifully for gusty no-flap landings.
'Brakes — check both sides' is the first taxi item.
Short-field: brakes held until full throttle, released together.

keyboard_mouse.usage_tip: Fuel selector (LEFT/RIGHT — no BOTH!), primer, and radios stay mouse-operated: the checklist wants you to look at the selector when you switch tanks.
'ATIS / altimeter — obtain / set' in Engine Start and Descent.
Freeze mid-flight and walk the emergency flows with the checklist app.
'Parking brake — SET' opens the run-up checklist.

keyboard_mouse.msfs_setting: SAVE CUSTOM CAMERA / LOAD CUSTOM CAMERA
keyboard_mouse.usage_tip: Save a fuel-selector camera — you'll use it every 30 minutes in cruise.
